Amarante '26 Week 03
Jose × Alvaro
01 02 03 04 Gallery
Week 03 1 – 7 JUN

03

Amarante local parties

Scroll
Festival

NIGTHS IN AMARANTE

Week three. The city had been building to this for days. Food stalls appearing overnight, strings of lights suddenly everywhere. Then on Thursday evening the Festa de São Gonçalo begins and Amarante remembers exactly who it is.

São Gonçalo, patron saint of marriages, has been celebrated here since the 12th century. The church that bears his name glows at night, and the processions that wind through the old streets carry centuries of tradition. But the festa is also deeply alive.

This week, the city of Amarante becomes a city full of celebrations and parties. Every corner of the city is filled with music, laughter and a lot of tradition.

We watched the fireworks over the river, tried the local delicacies and stayed up until late. It was a week of pure joy and celebration, a week that showed us the true spirit of Amarante and its people.

Our bosses, Joana and João, gave us a free day on friday 5th, so we could enjoy the festivities without worying about work. So we had a long weekend to explore the city, meet the locals and enjoy this nights.

We strongly recommend checking out this week photos at the gallery!
